title: knema stellata subsp. cryptocaryoides
intro: Knema stellata subsp. cryptocaryoides, also known as Borneo Nut, is a species of plant in the family Myristicaceae, native to Borneo. It has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help to prevent infections and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Borneo Nut include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of arthritis, as well as its ability to support immune function and provide antioxidant properties. Borneo Nut can be prepared as a herbal tea or extract, and it may also be used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever and digestive issues.
